sentences.arabic: يَتَمَيَّزُ الْمُؤْمِنُ بِقُوَّةِ تَوَكُّلِهِ عَلَى اللهِ تَعَالَى فِي السَّرَّاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ، مُوقِنًا أَنَّ جَمِيعَ الْأُمُورِ بِيَدِ الْخَالِقِ وَحْدَهُ.
sentences.translation: Vjernik se odlikuje snagom oslonca (tevekkula) na Uzvišenog Allaha u blagostanju i u nedaći, bivajući uvjeren da su sve stvari isključivo u rukama Stvoritelja.
sentences.translationEn: A believer is characterized by the strength of his trust (Tawakkul) in Allah the Almighty in prosperity and adversity, certain that all matters are in the hands of the Creator alone.

sentences.arabic: جَاءَ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنِ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: «عَجَبًا لِأَمْرِ الْمُؤْمِنِ، إِنَّ أَمْرَهُ كُلَّهُ خَيْرٌ، وَلَيْسَ ذَاكَ لِأَحَدٍ إِلَّا لِلْمُؤْمِنِ» [رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ فِي كِتَابِ الزُّهْدِ وَالرَّقَائِقِ، رَقْمُ ٢٩٩٩].
sentences.translation: U Sahihu Muslimu se prenosi od Poslanika, s.a.v.s.: 'Čudan li je primjer vjernika!
sentences.translationEn: It was stated in Sahih Muslim from the Prophet (PBUH): 'Wondrous is the affair of the believer, for all his affairs are good for him, and that is not for anyone except the believer' [Narrated by Muslim in the Book of Asceticism and Heart-Softeners, No. 2999].

sentences.arabic: يُفَسِّرُ شَيْخُ الْإِسْلَامِ ابْنُ تَيْمِيَّةَ هَذَا الْحَالَ بِأَنَّ الْإِيمَانَ يَنْقَسِمُ إِلَى نِصْفَيْنِ: نِصْفٌ صَبْرٌ وَنِصْفٌ شُكْرٌ.
sentences.translation: Svako njegovo stanje je dobro za njega, a to nije slučaj ni sa kim osim sa vjernikom' [Bilježi Muslim u Knjizi o zuhdu i suptilnostima, br. 2999].
sentences.translationEn: Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah explains this state by saying that faith is divided into two halves: half patience and half gratitude.

sentences.arabic: فَإِذَا أَصَابَتِ الْمُسْلِمَ نِعْمَةٌ شَكَرَ فَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَهُ، وَإِذَا أَصَابَتْهُ ضَرَّاءُ صَبَرَ فَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَهُ.
sentences.translation: Šejhul-islam Ibn Tejmijje objašnjava ovo stanje time da se iman dijeli na dvije polovine: polovinu strpljenja (sabr) i polovinu zahvalnosti (šukr).
sentences.translationEn: If a blessing reaches the Muslim, he is grateful and it is good for him, and if adversity strikes him, he is patient and it is good for him.

sentences.arabic: هَذَا التَّوَازُنُ النَّفْسِيُّ يَجْعَلُ الْمُسْلِمَ صَامِدًا أَمَامَ تَحَدِّيَاتِ الْحَيَاةِ، رَاضِيًا بِقَضَاءِ اللهِ وَقَدَرِهِ.
sentences.translation: Pa ako muslimana zadesi blagodat, on zahvali i to bude dobro po njega, a ako ga zadesi nedaća, on se strpi i to bude dobro po njega.
sentences.translationEn: This psychological balance makes the Muslim resilient in the face of life's challenges, satisfied with Allah's decree and predestination.

sentences.arabic: إِنَّ التَّوَكُّلَ الصَّادِقَ لَا يَعْنِي تَرْكَ الْأَسْبَابِ، بَلِ السَّعْيَ مَعَ تَعَلُّقِ الْقَلْبِ بِاللهِ.
sentences.translation: Ova psihička ravnoteža čini muslimana postojanim pred životnim izazovima, zadovoljnim Allahovom odredbom i kadrom. Iskreni tevekkul ne znači ostavljanje uzroka, već trud uz vezanost srca za Allaha.
sentences.translationEn: True trust does not mean abandoning means, but rather striving while the heart is attached to Allah.
